tasks.title: Establish the executable Rust foundation and command dispatch architecture
tasks.details: Replace the help-only bootstrap with a test-first command runtime, domain-oriented module layout, typed errors, and shared execution plumbing that can support the CLI, MCP server, and TUI.
steps: Create the core modules called for by the spec: manifest, lockfile, source, skill, overlay, adapter, planner, materialize, history, telemetry, doctor, mcp, and tui.
Add a dispatcher that maps parsed clap commands to typed handlers instead of stopping at help output.
Define shared error, response, and application-context types so later commands can reuse one execution model.

tasks.title: Implement `skillctl init` workspace bootstrap and repo integration
tasks.details: Create the manifest-free default workspace layout under `.agents/`, including optional local Git exclusions, without mutating unrelated repository files.
steps: Create `.agents/skills/`, `.agents/overlays/`, and a minimal `.agents/skillctl.yaml` with sensible defaults.
Add optional `.git/info/exclude` handling for generated runtime roots as described by the spec.
Return human-readable and JSON summaries describing what was created or skipped.

tasks.title: Implement manifest loading, validation, and serialization for `.agents/skillctl.yaml`
tasks.details: Model the workspace manifest with strong Rust types, explicit defaults, and validation for targets, projection policy, layout, imports, overrides, adapters, and telemetry settings.
steps: Parse the minimal and full manifest shapes described in the spec.
Validate projection policy, projection mode, layout paths, target names, and import definitions.
Support deterministic serialization so manifest output is stable in tests and Git diffs.

tasks.title: Implement lockfile modeling, deterministic IO, and state versioning
tasks.details: Add `.agents/skillctl.lock` support that records exact source identity, revision, hashes, timestamps, and effective-version data with deliberate schema evolution.
steps: Model lock entries for source type, normalized source URL, selected subpath, commit or digest, content hash, overlay hash, and effective version hash.
Read and write the lockfile deterministically so the same inputs produce the same file output.
Define versioning and migration hooks for future manifest, lockfile, and local-state changes.

tasks.title: Implement skill parsing and validation for `SKILL.md` plus vendor-specific metadata passthrough
tasks.details: Parse skills as directories containing `SKILL.md`, validate the open Agent Skills contract, and preserve vendor-specific extensions without mutating them.
steps: Validate that `SKILL.md` exists and that required fields such as name and description are present.
Check name and directory consistency, frontmatter parsing, and invalid path traversal in overlays.
Preserve supported extra metadata such as `agents/openai.yaml` and Claude-specific frontmatter fields.

tasks.title: Implement the adapter registry and target root planner for all supported runtimes
tasks.details: Encode runtime discovery roots, precedence rules, scope support, neutral-root compatibility, and symlink risk for `codex`, `claude-code`, `github-copilot`, `gemini-cli`, `amp`, and `opencode`.
steps: Define typed adapter metadata for workspace and user scopes, discovery roots, precedence behavior, and compatibility notes.
Implement the documented shared-root planning rules for neutral and native projection policies.
Expose planner results in a form that can be reused by sync, doctor, explain, and JSON output.

tasks.title: Implement source detection, normalization, and fetch support for Git, local paths, and archives
tasks.details: Support the install pipeline’s input sources, detect skills in the required directory layouts, and normalize candidates into a stable internal representation.
steps: Detect candidate skills in `.agents/skills/`, `.claude/skills/`, `.opencode/skills/`, and repo-root `skills/`.
Fetch or stage sources from Git URLs, local directories, and local archives while capturing immutable revision information.
Normalize detected candidates with source path, displayed name, compatibility hints, and selected subpath.

tasks.title: Implement effective-skill resolution, overlay application, and conflict handling
tasks.details: Resolve canonical local skills, imported skills, and overrides into one effective view per projected name while enforcing the precedence and tie rules in the spec.
steps: Build a resolver that combines manifest inputs, discovered local skills, imports from the lockfile, and overlays.
Apply explicit manifest priority first, then source-class precedence, and treat unresolved ties as errors.
Produce an explainable effective-skill graph that downstream commands can inspect.

tasks.title: Implement copy-first projection materialization and the `sync` command
tasks.details: Materialize effective skills into runtime-visible roots using deterministic copy mode by default, emit provenance metadata, and prune only prior `skillctl` outputs.
steps: Compute physical roots from the planner and materialize generated copies without touching canonical authoring directories.
Write `.skillctl-projection.json` metadata for each generated projection.
Implement stale-projection pruning that only removes directories previously generated by `skillctl`.

tasks.title: Implement the SQLite local state store and history ledger
tasks.details: Create `~/.skillctl/state.db` as the local system of record for installs, projections, modification detection, telemetry consent, pins, rollbacks, and history events.
steps: Design a versioned SQLite schema for install records, projection records, update checks, local modifications, pins, rollbacks, and telemetry settings.
Add a history-writing layer that records installs, updates, projections, detachments, and cleanup operations deterministically.
Expose query APIs that higher-level commands can use for history, explain, update planning, and TUI views.

tasks.title: Implement `skillctl install` end to end for interactive and non-interactive workflows
tasks.details: Support the main install journey from source inspection through selection, lockfile updates, projection, and history recording.
steps: Implement interactive selection that lists detected skills, locations, pinned revisions, and scope choices.
Implement exact-name non-interactive selection using `--name` or an equivalent selector that never guesses.
Pin the installed revision, update the lockfile and state store, run projection, and record the install event.

tasks.title: Implement the overlay model and `skillctl override` workflow
tasks.details: Add shadow-file overlays in `.agents/overlays/<skill-id>/` so imported skills can be customized without breaking upgradeability.
steps: Resolve overlay files by relative path and make them override upstream content in the effective-skill view.
Implement `skillctl override <skill>` to create the minimal editable overlay layout.
Track overlay hashes in the lockfile and state store so update and rollback logic can reason about drift.

tasks.title: Implement `skillctl update` planning, upstream checks, and local modification handling
tasks.details: Compare pinned installs with upstream revisions, detect overlays and unmanaged edits, and propose safe actions rather than overwriting state blindly.
steps: Check upstream sources and compare the installed pinned commit with the latest available revision.
Differentiate managed overlays, unmanaged edits to projected copies, and detached local forks.
Produce an update plan that can apply, defer, skip, or redirect the user into overlay or fork workflows while recording history.

tasks.title: Implement pinning, rollback, and fork or detach workflows
tasks.details: Let users pin revisions, inspect prior effective versions, roll back safely, and detach imported skills into canonical local ownership when needed.
steps: Implement `pin` and `rollback` against exact recorded versions in the lockfile and state database.
Implement `fork` to copy an effective skill into `.agents/skills/` and detach it from upstream updates.
Record every pin, rollback, and detach transition in local history and projection metadata.

tasks.title: Implement the remaining lifecycle and inventory commands
tasks.details: Fill out the day-to-day command surface for managed-skill visibility and maintenance.
steps: Implement `list`, `history`, and `path` using the manifest, lockfile, projections, and local state store.
Implement `enable`, `disable`, and `remove` so effective-skill selection and projections stay consistent.
Implement `clean` to remove only generated state and projections while never deleting canonical skills or overlays implicitly.

tasks.title: Implement `validate`, `doctor`, and `explain` diagnostics
tasks.details: Build diagnostics that explain why a skill is valid, missing, shadowed, stale, detached, or not loading for a target.
steps: Implement core validation for malformed skills, duplicate identities, invalid overlays, and target compatibility issues.
Implement `doctor` checks for shadowing, stale lockfile entries, unmanaged edits, wrong precedence roots, and adapter-specific loading problems.
Implement `explain` to show the active winner, shadowed candidates, physical roots, target visibility, and drift from pinned sources.

tasks.title: Implement opt-in symlink mode with adapter-specific warnings and safeguards
tasks.details: Keep copy mode as the default while supporting explicit symlink projection where the user opts in and the adapter deems it safe enough.
steps: Add projection-mode handling for `copy` and `symlink` in the planner and materializer.
Warn when selected targets are marked `symlink-unstable` and require explicit override behavior.
Report symlink risks consistently in human output, JSON output, and diagnostics.

tasks.title: Implement telemetry consent, settings commands, and public-only event emission
tasks.details: Add the first-run notice, opt-out controls, consent persistence, and event filtering so telemetry is useful without leaking private-source information.
steps: Implement `telemetry status`, `telemetry enable`, and `telemetry disable` backed by the local state store.
Show a clear first-run notice and persist user consent separately from local history so history still works when telemetry is off.
Emit only public-source install and update events and suppress remote telemetry for private or local-only sources.

tasks.title: Implement the bundled `skillctl` management skill at user scope
tasks.details: Ship and manage a built-in `skillctl` skill that helps agents use the CLI and debug missing or stale skills.
steps: Package the bundled skill as a managed built-in asset with explicit versioning.
Install and keep it updated in user scope without conflicting with user-managed skills.
Require explicit user action for removal and surface its state in history and diagnostics.

tasks.title: Normalize global flags, JSON output, exit codes, and non-interactive behavior across the CLI
tasks.details: Make the command surface stable for humans and agents by standardizing `--json`, `--quiet`, `--verbose`, `--no-input`, `--interactive`, `--name`, `--scope`, `--target`, and `--cwd` semantics everywhere.
steps: Define one response envelope that every command and subcommand uses for success, warnings, errors, and data.
Implement the documented exit-code contract, including warning and validation-specific exits and interactive-input-required failures.
Audit every command so interactive features have deterministic non-interactive behavior and machine-readable output.

tasks.title: Implement trust states and security warnings for untrusted or risky skills
tasks.details: Add the trust model needed to identify risky installs, script-bearing skills from untrusted sources, and operations that should be blocked or downgraded.
steps: Model trust state for sources and effective skills so install, update, and doctor can reason about risk.
Warn or block operations involving risky content according to the spec’s trust-gate and exit-code expectations.
Surface trust decisions in diagnostics, JSON responses, and local history.

tasks.title: Implement the MCP server and v1 tool parity
tasks.details: Expose the lifecycle manager to agents through MCP with the same behavior and structured output as the CLI.
steps: Implement `mcp serve` and the v1 tools: `skills_list`, `skills_install`, `skills_remove`, `skills_sync`, `skills_update`, `skills_rollback`, `skills_history`, `skills_explain`, `skills_override_create`, `skills_validate`, `skills_doctor`, and `skills_telemetry_status`.
Reuse the same typed command layer as the CLI so behavior and JSON output stay in lockstep.
Require explicit parameters for destructive actions and avoid hidden mutations outside managed roots.

tasks.title: Implement the terminal UI for inspection, updates, and history
tasks.details: Build the optional TUI around the same state and command model used by the CLI so it adds visibility without adding a second behavior surface.
steps: Show installed versions, update availability, overlays, local modifications, target visibility, and rollback or pin details.
Map every TUI action to an existing CLI command instead of inventing TUI-only behavior.
Keep TUI reads and writes deterministic and safe for concurrent CLI usage.

tasks.title: Build the full test suite, fixture repos, and cross-platform integration matrix
tasks.details: Added fixture-backed integration coverage for the spec matrix across workspace and user scope, reusable fixture repos for source layouts and failure cases, and telemetry source suppression tests.
steps: Add unit tests for manifest parsing, lockfile roundtrips, overlay resolution, conflict resolution, root planning, install candidate detection, history writes, telemetry suppression, and JSON output contracts.

Create fixture repos for canonical local skills, imported skills with overlays, same-name conflicts, monorepos, broken symlink cases, alternate source layouts, update blockers, and private-source installs.
Run integration coverage across OS, mode, scope, and target combinations called out in the spec.

tasks.title: Add CI quality gates and documentation verification
tasks.details: Enforce the engineering discipline in the spec so warnings, formatting drift, missing docs, and broken tests are treated as defects.
steps: Add CI jobs for `cargo fmt --check`, strict `clippy`, `cargo test`, and documentation verification.
Fail CI on warnings and ensure public modules, types, and important functions are covered by Rust docs.
Make the automated checks align with the growing unit and integration test suite.

tasks.title: Package the binary and release a curl-installable distribution path
tasks.details: Turn the Rust binary into a shippable product with versioned releases and an installation flow that matches the product shape in the spec.
steps: Define release artifacts for supported platforms and ensure the binary bundles required built-in assets.
Add a repeatable release workflow that publishes tagged binaries and supports curl-based installation.
Verify release builds preserve deterministic behavior and sane first-run setup.

tasks.title: Write end-user documentation, command help, and troubleshooting guides
tasks.details: Document the model, command surface, and debugging flows so a new user can understand the system quickly and recover from common failures with one command.
steps: Expand the README and command help to explain canonical roots, imports, overlays, projections, history, telemetry, and trust states.
Add examples for install, update, rollback, doctor, explain, MCP usage, and TUI equivalents.
Document how to diagnose missing, stale, shadowed, detached, or conflicting skills across supported runtimes.
